6) State Level Technical Advisory Committee. The minutes of the meeting on 02.02.2026, No. Seat-2026 / P.No.11 / Papu-13, D. 25.03.2026
The Proposal: -

Buldhana district. Deulghat Dhar and 5 villages. Buldana (Scheme Code - 20091206) The original budget of the Regional Water Supply Scheme was prepared by the Chief Engineer, Maharashtra Life Authority Regional Division, Amravati vide Reference No. Technical approval is provided under Section 2. The estimated cost of the project is Rs. 3127.83 is the focus. Also, Reference No. Under Section 1, administrative approval has been granted for a price as low as Rs 2860.58 lakh. Also, Reference No. 5 of the Department. The tender for the additional rate of the scheme has been operationally approved vide letter dated 15.06.2026. The Chief Engineer, Maharashtra Life Authority, Amravati proposed to grant revised administrative approval to the proposed Regional Water Supply Scheme vide Reference Letter No. 4 here. The letter dated 18.09.2024 has been submitted to the government. Reference No. 3 here. Under the letter dated 18.09.2024, the government was under consideration to grant revised administrative approval to the price which would be in accordance with the revised technical approval given by the Chief Engineer, Maharashtra Life Authority Regional Division, Amravati.
The government's decision:
"Jal Jeevan Mission Programme" in Buldana district. Deulghat Dhar and 5 villages. Buldana Regional Water Supply Project Receives Tender for Goods and Services Tax (GST) Tariff Excess Rate
Government Decision No: Suprama-2026 / P.No.69 / Papu-13

Also, due to technical changes, the price of the scheme has gone up, subject to the following conditions: Rs. The revised price of 2954.44 lakh (Rupees Nineteen Crore Fifty Four Lakh Forty Four Thousand only) is being approved by the Administrator.
Conditions
1) In pursuance of this scheme, Reference No. 6 Necessary action should be taken on the suggestions made in the history of the State Level Technical Advisory Committee here.
2) The CEO / Chief Engineer will be responsible for completing all the remaining works within the prescribed time frame at the above mentioned revised cost.
3) The terms and conditions mentioned while granting the revised technical approval as well as the original administrative approval agreed upon for the scheme should be strictly adhered to.

4) On completion of the scheme, it should be handed over to the concerned Gram Panchayat. The concerned Gram Panchayat / Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority shall be responsible for taking over the scheme within one month from the date of receipt of intimation from the concerned Executive Engineer, Rural Water Supply Department, Zila Parishad that the scheme has been actually completed. Immediately after the actual completion of the scheme, a joint inspection of the scheme should be conducted by the concerned Executive Engineer, Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority and the concerned Gram Panchayat officials and if any defects are found in such inspection, they should be rectified before handing over the scheme.

5) The Gram Panchayat / Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority concerned shall not receive any assistance from the Government for meeting the annual expenditure towards implementation of the scheme, maintenance, repairs (including special modifications), depreciation in the scheme or renovation of machinery.

6) On completion of the scheme, it will be mandatory for the local body (concerned Gram Panchayat / Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority) to implement the scheme. The accounts of the scheme should also be closed.

7) According to a CAG report in 2013, 21 per cent of water supply schemes in the state have been shut down due to poor maintenance / non-payment of electricity bills due to lack of these maintenance repairs. In fact, in order to make the scheme financially viable, it will be the responsibility of the concerned Gram Panchayat / Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority to increase the water tariff as per requirement.
8) Action should be taken in accordance with the prevailing policy on extortion.
9) It shall be the responsibility of the Zilla Parishad / Maharashtra Jeevan Authority that the present scheme shall not be revised under any circumstances.

10) Since no administrative expenditure is admissible under the Jal Jeevan Mission, it should be excluded if 4% of the administrative expenditure has been taken up in the revised budget of the scheme.

and to be shared from the sanctioned grant of the State share.
